'''Erilnar''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories's]] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. Serving as the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Centrality, Erilnar had three hundred thousand [[Water/Legends|lakes]] on its surface, which forced the planet's transportation to go underwater. At some point by the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[terrorist attack on Erilnar]] destroyed most of the world's [[Monorail/Legends|monorail]] network and resulted in a million [[Death/Legends|casualties]]. Blaming the Empire for its non-reaction to the attack, the Erilnar [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Kulbart Zamoon]] joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Description==
Erilnar was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Gamer" /> located in the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]], a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The [[Cadma Conduit]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] connected it to the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon]] and [[Scillal system/Legends|Scillal systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> The landmass of Erilnar was broken up by three hundred thousand [[Water/Legends|lakes]], thus making it very different from<ref name="Gamer" /> the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city-covered]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TEA" /> Erilnar's unusual geography forced transportation, which included a [[Monorail/Legends|monorail]] system, to go underwater.<ref name="Gamer" />

==History==
At some point, Erilnar became the capital world of the Centrality,<ref name="Gamer" /> a coalition of planets established in [[3350 BBY]]. On Erilnar, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] dominated the Centrality government, which came to be manipulated behind the scenes by [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] based in nearby [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="TEA" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]],<ref name="Empire">{{InsiderCite|146|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: Clearance for Immediate Launch}} establishes that Erilnar was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] participant [[Kulbart Zamoon]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' further places that battle during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].</ref> Erilnar was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of<ref name="Rogues">{{InsiderCite|146|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: Clearance for Immediate Launch}}</ref> the Human<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> [[Kulbart Zamoon]].<ref name="Rogues" /> Around that time, [[Terrorist attack on Erilnar|one]] in a series of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] attacks on Erilnar—an incident that saw the destruction of most of the planet's monorail network—[[Death/Legends|claimed]] the lives of a million people.<ref name="Gamer" /> When the Empire,<ref name="Rogues" /> with which the Centrality had allied itself as a semi-independent territory<ref name="Gamer" /> did not interfere in the aftermath of the attack, Zamoon held it accountable and joined its enemy, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], as a result.<ref name="Rogues" />

==Behind the scenes==
Erilnar was first mentioned in the article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]," which was authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]<ref name="Gamer" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square T-8.<ref name="TEA" />
